seoTitle: How to Add a Clear All Filters Button in Power BI
metaDescription: Add a one-click Clear All Filters button in Power BI so viewers can reset every applied filter instantly instead of adjusting each one manually.
intro: When a report has several filters applied, resetting them one at a time is tedious, especially during a live presentation. This guide covers how to add a Clear All Filters button that resets everything with a single click. It's a small addition that makes navigating complex datasets noticeably smoother.

steps.heading: Understand the Clear All Filters concept
steps.body: Recognize that this feature gives viewers a single control to reset every applied filter at once, rather than clearing them individually.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step setup
steps.body: Add the button to your report and configure it so it clears the active filter selections when clicked.

steps.heading: Demonstrate the one-click reset feature
steps.body: Apply several filters, then test the button to confirm it restores the report to its unfiltered state instantly.

steps.heading: Understand the benefits for data analysis
steps.body: Recognize how this feature speeds up analysis and reporting by removing the friction of manually clearing multiple filters.

steps.heading: Apply it across beginner and advanced dashboards
steps.body: Use this button in both simple and complex reports, since any dashboard with multiple filters can benefit from a quick reset option.

faq.question: How is this different from resetting individual filters?
faq.answer: A Clear All Filters button removes every applied filter at once, instead of requiring you to clear each filter one by one.

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to add this button?
faq.answer: No, it can be built using standard Power BI features without needing custom code.

faq.question: Will the button also clear filters set through slicers?
faq.answer: Depending on how it's configured, it can be set up to reset slicer selections along with other applied filters.

faq.question: Can I use this button on every page of my report?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add and configure the button separately on each page where you want a one-click reset option available.

faq.question: Is this feature helpful during live presentations?
faq.answer: Yes, it's especially useful in presentations where quickly returning to the default view keeps things moving smoothly for the audience.
